name: Svg.Chart

aliases.name: Bar
aliases.type: { attributes : List.List (Svg.Attribute msg), width : Basics.Float, position : Basics.Float, value : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: GroupBar
aliases.type: { attributes : List.List (Svg.Attribute msg), width : Basics.Float, value : Basics.Float }

values.name: aura
values.comment:
Make a shape with a little translucent border.

    someDot : Svg msg
    someDot =
      aura radius width opacity circle "blue" plane x y
values.type: Basics.Float -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Chart.Shape -> String.String -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg

values.name: bars
values.comment:
You can draw a bar chart like this:

    main : Svg msg
    main =
      let toBars ( a, b ) =
            [ bar [ fill "red" ] 0.8 a
            , bar [ fill "blue" ] 0.8 b
            ]
      in
      svg
        [ width (String.fromFloat plane.x.length)
        , height (String.fromFloat plane.y.length)
        ]
        [ bars plane toBars [ ( 2, 3 ), ( 5, 1 ), ( 1, 5 ) ]
        ]

Note on `width`: The width takes catersian units, however, should you have
a width in SVG units, you can use `Svg.Coordinates.scaleCartesian` to
translate it into cartesian units.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (data -> List.List (Svg.Chart.GroupBar msg)) -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: circle
values.type: Svg.Chart.Shape

values.name: clear
values.comment: A dot without visual representation.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: container
values.comment:
Container for your chart, in case you use the `eventCatcher`.
Without this, your coordinates from the events will be wrong!
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: cross
values.type: Svg.Chart.Shape

values.name: decodePoint
values.comment:
Decode position of mouse when tracking events. The `Plane` may
change due to size changes of the viewport, so make sure to use the
one given in the second argument. Also, make sure to wrap your chart
in `container` otherwise your read coordinates maybe wrong!
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Svg.Chart.Point -> msg) -> Json.Decode.Decoder msg

values.name: diamond
values.type: Svg.Chart.Shape

values.name: disconnected
values.comment:
Make a shape with a white border.

    someDot : Svg msg
    someDot =
      disconnected radius width circle "blue" plane x y
values.type: Basics.Float -> Basics.Int -> Svg.Chart.Shape -> String.String -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg

values.name: empty
values.comment:
Make a shape with a white core.

    someDot : Svg msg
    someDot =
      empty radius width circle "blue" plane x y
values.type: Basics.Float -> Basics.Int -> Svg.Chart.Shape -> String.String -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg

values.name: eventCatcher
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: frame
values.comment:
Create an id to use in a clip path to prevent elements from going into your margins.

    svg []
      [ frame "some-id" plane
      , g [ clipPath "url(#some-id)" ] [ stuffThatShouldntGoIntoTheMargin ]
      ]
values.type: String.String -> Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: full
values.comment:
Make a regular shape.

    someDot : Svg msg
    someDot =
      full radius circle "blue" plane x y
values.type: Basics.Float -> Svg.Chart.Shape -> String.String -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg

values.name: getNearest
values.comment:
Get the data coordinates nearest to the event.
Returns `Nothing` if you have no data showing.
values.type: List.List (Svg.Chart.DataPoint data) -> Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Svg.Chart.Point -> Maybe.Maybe data

values.name: getNearestX
values.comment: Get the data coordinates horizontally nearest to the event.
values.type: List.List (Svg.Chart.DataPoint data) -> Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Svg.Chart.Point -> List.List data

values.name: getWithin
values.comment:
Get the data coordinates nearest of the event within the radius
you provide in the first argument. Returns `Nothing` if you have no data showing.
values.type: Basics.Float -> List.List (Svg.Chart.DataPoint data) -> Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Svg.Chart.Point -> Maybe.Maybe data

values.name: getWithinX
values.comment:
Finds the data coordinates horizontally nearest to the event, within the
distance you provide in the first argument.
values.type: Basics.Float -> List.List (Svg.Chart.DataPoint data) -> Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Svg.Chart.Point -> List.List data

values.name: histogram
values.comment:
Make a histogram.

    main : Svg msg
    main =
      let toBars _ datum _ =
            [ Bar [] 10 datum.timestamp datum.score ]
      in
      svg
        [ width (String.fromFloat plane.x.length)
        , height (String.fromFloat plane.y.length)
        ]
        [ histogram plane toBars [ ( 1, 1 ), ( 2, 2 ), ( 3, 6 ) ]
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (Maybe.Maybe data -> data -> Maybe.Maybe data -> List.List (Svg.Chart.Bar msg)) -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: horizontal
values.comment:
Renders a horizontal line.

    myLine : Svg msg
    myLine =
      horizontal plane [ stroke "pink" ] y x0 x1
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: isXPastMiddle
values.comment: Is x past the middle of the chart?
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Bool

values.name: line
values.comment:
Renders a line.

    myLine : Svg msg
    myLine =
      line plane [ stroke "pink" ] x0 y0 x1 y1
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: linear
values.comment: Series with linear interpolation.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> (data -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg) -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: linearArea
values.comment: Area series with linear interpolation.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> (data -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg) -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.comment: Get the midmost x coordinate.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float

values.name: middleOfY
values.comment: Get the midmost y coordinate.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float

values.name: monotone
values.comment: Series with monotone interpolation.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> (data -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg) -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: monotoneArea
values.comment: Area series with monotone interpolation.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> (data -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg) -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: plus
values.type: Svg.Chart.Shape

values.name: position
values.comment: Place some SVG on specific coordinates.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Attribute msg

values.name: positionHtml
values.comment:
Place some Html on specific coordinates. You must wrap your chart
in `container` for it to work! Remember also to put your html elements outside
your `svg` element, otherwise they won't work!

      positionHtml plane x y xOff yOff
        [ style "font-size" "14px" ]
        [ text "Hello!" ]
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: responsive
values.comment: Set the dimensions of your chart, but let it respond to size changes in parent.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Svg.Attribute msg

values.name: scatter
values.comment:
Series with no interpolation.

    someScatter : Svg msg
    someScatter =
      scatter plane .x .y (full 6 circle "blue") points
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> Svg.Chart.Dot msg) -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: square
values.type: Svg.Chart.Shape

values.name: static
values.comment: Set the size of your chart.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg)

values.name: toDataPoints
values.type: (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> List.List data -> List.List (Svg.Chart.DataPoint data)

values.name: tooltip
values.comment:
A basic tooltip.

      tooltip plane x y
        [ style "font-size" "14px" ]
        [ Html.text "I'm a tooltip!" ]
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: triangle
values.type: Svg.Chart.Shape

values.name: vertical
values.comment:
Renders a vertical line.

    myLine : Svg msg
    myLine =
      vertical plane [ stroke "pink" ] x y0 y1
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: xArrow
values.comment:
Place an arrow pointing to the right somewhere.

      xArrow plane color x y xOff yOff
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> String.String -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: xAxis
values.comment:
Renders a horizontal line with the full length of the range.

    myXAxisOrGridLine : Svg msg
    myXAxisOrGridLine =
      xAxis plane [] yPosition
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: xGrid
values.comment: Renders a horizontal line with the full length of the range.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: xLabel
values.comment:
Renders a label for the horizontal axis.

    horizontalLabel : Svg msg
    horizontalLabel =
      xLabel "blue" .timestamp format plane y datum
values.type: List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> String.String) -> Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: xLabels
values.comment:
Renders labels for the horizontal axis.

    horizontalLabels : Svg msg
    horizontalLabels =
      xLabels plane (xLabel "blue" .timestamp format) axisYCoordinate data
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> data -> Svg.Svg msg) -> Basics.Float -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: xTick
values.comment: Renders a single tick for the horizontal axis.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Int -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: xTicks
values.comment:
Renders ticks for the horizontal axis.

    horizontalTicks : Svg msg
    horizontalTicks =
      xTicks plane height [ stroke "pink" ] axisYCoordinate tickPositions
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Int -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> List.List Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: yArrow
values.comment:
Place an arrow pointing up somewhere.

      yArrow plane color x y xOff yOff
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> String.String -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: yAxis
values.comment:
Renders a vertical line with the full length of the domain.

    myYAxisOrGridLine : Svg msg
    myYAxisOrGridLine =
      yAxis plane [] xPosition
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: yGrid
values.comment: Renders a horizontal line with the full length of the domain.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: yLabel
values.comment:
Renders a label for the vertical axis.

    verticalLabel : Svg msg
    verticalLabel =
      yLabel "blue" .age format plane x y
values.type: List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> (data -> Basics.Float) -> (data -> String.String) -> Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: yLabels
values.comment:
Renders labels for the vertical axis.

    verticalLabels : Svg msg
    verticalLabels =
      yLabels plane (yLabel "blue" .age format) axisXCoordinate data
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> (Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> data -> Svg.Svg msg) -> Basics.Float -> List.List data -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: yTick
values.comment: Renders a single tick for the vertical axis.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Int -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: yTicks
values.comment:
Renders ticks for the vertical axis.

    verticalTicks : Svg msg
    verticalTicks =
      yTicks plane width [ stroke "pink" ] axisXCoordinate tickPositions
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Int -> List.List (Svg.Attribute msg) -> Basics.Float -> List.List Basics.Float -> Svg.Svg msg

name: Svg.Coordinates
comment:
This module contains helpers for cartesian/SVG coordinate translation.

# Plane
@docs Plane, Axis

# Plane from data

You may want to produce a plane which fits all your data. For that you need
to find the minimum and maximum values withing your data in order to calculate
the domain and range.

@docs minimum, maximum

    planeFromPoints : List Point -> Plane
    planeFromPoints points =
      { x =
        { marginLower = 10
        , marginUpper = 10
        , length = 300
        , min = minimum .x points
        , max = maximum .x points
        }
      , y =
        { marginLower = 10
        , marginUpper = 10
        , length = 300
        , min = minimum .y points
        , max = maximum .y points
        }
      }

# Cartesian to SVG
@docs toSVGX, toSVGY, scaleSVG

# SVG to cartesian
@docs toCartesianX, toCartesianY, scaleCartesian

# Helpers
@docs place, placeWithOffset

aliases.name: Axis
aliases.comment:
The axis of the plane.

  - The margin properties are the upper and lower margins for the axis. So for example,
    if you want to add margin on top of the plot, increase the marginUpper of
    the y-`Axis`.
  - The length is the length of your SVG axis. (`plane.x.length` is the width,
    `plane.y.length` is the height)
  - The `min` and `max` values is the reach of your plane. (Domain for the y-axis, range
    for the x-axis)
aliases.type: { marginLower : Basics.Float, marginUpper : Basics.Float, length : Basics.Float, min : Basics.Float, max : Basics.Float }

values.name: maximum
values.comment: Helper to extract the maximum value amongst your coordinates.
values.type: List.List (a -> Basics.Float) -> List.List a -> Basics.Float

values.name: minimum
values.comment: Helper to extract the minimum value amongst your coordinates.
values.type: List.List (a -> Basics.Float) -> List.List a -> Basics.Float

values.name: place
values.comment:
A `transform translate(x, y)` SVG attribute. Beware that using this and
  and another transform attribute on the same node, will overwrite the first.
  If that's the case, just make one yourself:

    myTransformAttribute : Svg.Attribute msg
    myTransformAttribute =
      transform <|
        "translate("
        ++ String.fromFloat (toSVGX plane x) ++ ","
        ++ String.fromFloat (toSVGY plane y) ++ ") "
        ++ "rotateX(" ++ whatever ++ ")"
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Attribute msg

values.name: placeWithOffset
values.comment: Place at coordinate, but you may add a SVG offset. See `place` above for important notes.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float -> Svg.Attribute msg

values.name: scaleCartesian
values.comment:
For scaling a SVG value to a cartesian value. Note that this will _not_
  return a coordinate on the plane, but the scaled value.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Axis -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float

values.name: scaleSVG
values.comment:
For scaling a cartesian value to a SVG value. Note that this will _not_
  return a coordinate on the plane, but the scaled value.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Axis -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float

values.name: toCartesianX
values.comment: Translate a cartesian x-coordinate to its SVG x-coordinate.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float

values.name: toCartesianY
values.comment: Translate a cartesian y-coordinate to its SVG y-coordinate.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float

values.name: toSVGX
values.comment: Translate a SVG x-coordinate to its cartesian x-coordinate.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float

values.name: toSVGY
values.comment: Translate a SVG y-coordinate to its cartesian y-coordinate.
values.type: Svg.Coordinates.Plane -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float

name: Svg.Tiles

aliases.name: Map
aliases.comment: (You can use the helpers for calculating most of these properties. You're welcome.)
aliases.type: { tiles : List.List (Svg.Tiles.Tile msg), tilesPerRow : Basics.Int, tileWidth : Basics.Float, tileHeight : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: Tile
aliases.type: { content : Maybe.Maybe (Svg.Svg msg), attributes : List.List (Svg.Attribute msg), index : Basics.Int }

values.name: proportion
values.comment: For heatmaps. It calculates a value's value relative to all values.
values.type: (a -> Basics.Float) -> List.List a -> Basics.Float -> Basics.Float

values.name: tileHeight
values.comment:
Pass the __height__ of your map, the __amount of
 tiles in a row__, and the __total number of tiles__,
 and it gives you back the height of a single tile.
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int

values.name: tileWidth
values.comment:
Pass the __width__ of your map, and the __amount of
 tiles in a row__, and it gives you back the width of
 a single tile.
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int

values.name: tileXCoord
values.comment:
Pass the __tile width__, the __amount of tiles in a row__, and
 the __tile's index__ and it'll get you it's x-coordinate.
values.type: Basics.Float -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Float

values.name: tileYCoord
values.comment:
Pass the __tile height__, the __amount of tiles in a row__ and
 the __tile's index__ and it'll get you it's y-coordinate.
values.type: Basics.Float -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Float

values.name: view
values.comment: View a map!
values.type: Svg.Tiles.Map msg -> Svg.Svg msg
